annotations.label: Finite difference

anchor.snippet: A finite difference is a mathematical expression of the form

annotations.note: The forward-difference case `f(x+h) - f(x)` is defined as `fwdDiff h f` with notation `Δ_[h]`.

annotations.label: Difference operator

anchor.snippet: is the operator that maps a function f to the function

annotations.note: Bundled as the ℤ-linear endomorphism `fwdDiffₗ M G h : Module.End ℤ (M → G)`.

annotations.label: Difference equation

anchor.snippet: A difference equation is a functional equation that involves the finite difference operator

annotations.note: No dedicated notion of `difference equation` (functional equation involving Δ) found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Forward difference

anchor.snippet: A forward difference , denoted

annotations.note: Definition `fwdDiff h f n = f (n + h) - f n` matches the Wikipedia forward difference exactly.

annotations.label: Backward difference

anchor.snippet: A backward difference uses the function values at x and x − h

annotations.note: No `backwardDiff`/`bwdDiff` declaration found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Central difference

anchor.snippet: Finally, the central difference is given by

annotations.note: No `centralDiff` or symmetric-difference operator declaration found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Derivative as a limit

anchor.snippet: The derivative of a function f at a point x is defined by the limit

annotations.note: `HasDerivAt f f' x ↔ Tendsto (slope f x) (𝓝[≠] x) (𝓝 f')`, with `slope f x y = (y-x)⁻¹ • (f y - f x)`, is the difference-quotient limit definition.

annotations.label: Forward difference approximates derivative

anchor.snippet: Hence, the forward difference divided by h approximates the derivative when h is small

annotations.note: Characterizes the derivative as the limit of `(f(x+h) - f(x))/h` as `h → 0`, i.e. the forward-difference quotient.

annotations.label: Backward difference approximation

anchor.snippet: The same formula holds for the backward difference

annotations.note: The two-sided slope-limit characterization covers `h → 0⁻` (backward) as well as `h → 0⁺`.

annotations.label: Central difference is more accurate

anchor.snippet: However, the central (also called centered) difference yields a more accurate approximation

annotations.note: No statement about higher-order accuracy of the central-difference approximation found.

annotations.label: Oscillating function with zero central difference

anchor.snippet: If f ( nh ) = 1 for n odd, and f ( nh ) = 2 for n even

annotations.note: Illustrative example; no corresponding formal statement in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Central difference approximation of second derivative

anchor.snippet: we obtain the central difference approximation of the second derivative

annotations.note: Mathlib does not state the `(f(x+h) - 2f(x) + f(x-h))/h²` approximation to `f''`.

annotations.label: n-th order forward, backward, and central differences

anchor.snippet: the n -th order forward, backward, and central differences are given by

annotations.note: `Δ_[h]^[n] f` (iterated forward difference) is defined and given the explicit binomial formula; backward/central are absent.

annotations.label: Binomial transform via forward differences

anchor.snippet: Forward differences applied to a sequence are sometimes called the binomial transform of the sequence

annotations.note: The explicit alternating-sum-of-shifts formula (the binomial-transform identity) is `fwdDiff_iter_eq_sum_shift`; the `binomial transform` name is not used.

annotations.label: Second-order accurate first-derivative combination

anchor.snippet: approximates f ′( x ) up to a term of order h 2

annotations.note: No O(h²) error-bound combination of forward differences approximating `f'` in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Seminumerical differentiation of second derivative

anchor.snippet: the latter can be approximated by second-order central difference of f ′( x )

annotations.note: No central-difference approximation to `f''` formalized.

annotations.label: n-th pairwise difference of degree-n polynomial

anchor.snippet: After n pairwise differences, the following result can be achieved

annotations.note: `Δ_[1]^[P.natDegree] P.eval = P.leadingCoeff • P.natDegree!` is exactly the `n!·a_n` result.

annotations.label: Base case for polynomial difference

anchor.snippet: Let Q ( x ) be a polynomial of degree 1

annotations.note: Implicit base case (`n = 1`) of the formalized factorial identity; not stated as a separate lemma.

annotations.label: Inductive step for polynomial difference

anchor.snippet: Let R ( x ) be a polynomial of degree m − 1 where m ≥ 2

annotations.note: The inductive proof step is folded into the proof of `Polynomial.fwdDiff_iter_degree_eq_factorial`; not a standalone lemma.

annotations.label: Finding polynomial via differences table

anchor.snippet: This identity can be used to find the lowest-degree polynomial that intercepts a number of points

annotations.note: Interpolation-by-differences-table example has no dedicated Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Power and Leibniz rule for differences

anchor.snippet: For all positive k and n

annotations.note: The Leibniz/product rule `Δ(fg) = Δf · g + f · Δg + Δf · Δg` is `fwdDiff_smul`; no power rule for differences is stated.

annotations.label: Leibniz rule for differences

anchor.snippet: Leibniz rule

annotations.note: The discrete Leibniz rule `Δ_[h](f • g) x = Δf x • g x + f x • Δg x + Δf x • Δg x` is `fwdDiff_smul`.

annotations.label: Newton forward difference / Gregory–Newton interpolation

anchor.snippet: The Newton series consists of the terms of the Newton forward difference equation

annotations.note: `f(y + n•h) = ∑ k ∈ range (n+1), n.choose k • Δ_[h]^[k] f y` is labelled the Gregory–Newton formula.

annotations.label: Sine function and Newton series

anchor.snippet: the sine function vanishes at integer multiples of

annotations.note: No Mathlib statement of the Newton series for `sin`.

annotations.label: Doubling Fibonacci sequence Newton formula

anchor.snippet: consider the first few terms of doubling the Fibonacci sequence

annotations.note: Specific Fibonacci-doubling example is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Divided differences for nonuniform steps

anchor.snippet: For the case of nonuniform steps in the values of x , Newton computes the divided differences

annotations.note: No `dividedDifference` / Newton's divided differences declaration found.

annotations.label: Mahler's theorem

anchor.snippet: Mahler's theorem states that the assumption that f is a polynomial function can be weakened

annotations.note: Mahler's theorem is `PadicInt.hasSum_mahler`: every continuous `f : C(ℤ_[p], E)` equals the sum `∑ mahlerTerm (Δ_[1]^[n] f 0) n`.

annotations.label: Carlson's theorem

anchor.snippet: Carlson's theorem provides necessary and sufficient conditions for a Newton series to be unique

annotations.note: No Carlson's theorem (uniqueness of entire-function interpolation) in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Difference operator as shift minus identity

anchor.snippet: The forward difference can be considered as an operator , called the difference operator

annotations.note: Defined as `shiftₗ M G h := fwdDiffₗ M G h + 1`, so `fwdDiffₗ = shiftₗ - 1`, exactly Δ = T − I.

annotations.label: Higher-order differences recursively

anchor.snippet: The finite difference of higher orders can be defined in recursive manner

annotations.note: Higher-order iteration is `Δ_[h]^[n]` via `Function.iterate`, with explicit form in `fwdDiff_iter_eq_sum_shift`.

annotations.label: Linearity of difference operator

anchor.snippet: The difference operator Δ h is a linear operator

annotations.note: Bundled as a `Module.End ℤ (M → G)`; ingredients are `fwdDiff_add` and `fwdDiff_const_smul`.

annotations.label: Operator equation T_h = e^{hD}

anchor.snippet: Formally applying the Taylor series with respect to h , yields the operator equation

annotations.note: The formal operator identity `T_h = e^{hD}` (or `Δ = e^{hD} − 1`) is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Umbral sine

anchor.snippet: For example, the umbral sine is

annotations.note: No umbral calculus / umbral sine in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Umbral eigenfunction is exponential

anchor.snippet: the eigenfunction of

annotations.note: No statement that exponentials are eigenfunctions of `Δ_h`.

annotations.label: Dirac delta umbral correspondent

anchor.snippet: the Dirac delta function maps to its umbral correspondent, the cardinal sine function

annotations.note: No umbral correspondence Dirac↔sinc in Mathlib (sinc exists but not in this connection).

annotations.label: Antidifference operator

anchor.snippet: The inverse operator of the forward difference operator

annotations.note: No indefinite-sum / antidifference operator declared in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constant and Linearity rules

anchor.section: Rules for calculus of finite difference operators
anchor.snippet: Constant rule : If c is a constant

annotations.note: `fwdDiff_const` (Δ of constants is 0) plus `fwdDiff_add` and `fwdDiff_const_smul` give the constant/linearity rules.

annotations.label: Product rule for differences

anchor.section: Rules for calculus of finite difference operators
anchor.snippet: Product rule

annotations.note: `Δ_[h] (f • g) = Δf • g + f • Δg + Δf • Δg`, exactly the discrete product rule.

annotations.label: Quotient rule for differences

anchor.section: Rules for calculus of finite difference operators
anchor.snippet: Quotient rule

annotations.note: No finite-difference quotient rule found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Summation rules for differences

anchor.section: Rules for calculus of finite difference operators
anchor.snippet: Summation rules

annotations.note: Linearity over finite sums (`Δ` commutes with `∑`) is `fwdDiff_finsetSum`; telescoping / fundamental-theorem-style summation identities are not stated.

annotations.note: No general weighted-stencil `∑ μᵢ f(x + i h)` operator declared.

annotations.label: Möbius inversion generalization

anchor.snippet: Difference operator generalizes to Möbius inversion over a partially ordered set

annotations.note: The incidence-algebra Möbius function `IncidenceAlgebra.mu` realizes Möbius inversion on a poset.

annotations.note: No partial-derivative finite-difference stencils declared.

anchor.snippet: evaluating the full

annotations.note: Computational-cost analysis is outside Mathlib's scope.

annotations.label: Finite difference method

anchor.snippet: The resulting methods are called finite difference methods

annotations.note: No general framework of finite-difference methods for numerical solution of (P)DEs in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Falling factorial / Pochhammer symbol

anchor.snippet: is the " falling factorial " or "lower factorial"

annotations.note: The falling factorial `(x)_k = x(x-1)...(x-k+1)` is `descPochhammer` (with `ascPochhammer` for the rising-factorial variant).

annotations.label: Chu–Vandermonde identity

anchor.snippet: the Chu–Vandermonde identity

annotations.note: Vandermonde's identity `(m + n).choose k = ∑ ij ∈ antidiagonal k, m.choose ij.1 * n.choose ij.2` is `Nat.add_choose_eq`.
